page: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a memory-optimized server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with 32 physical cores and a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong memory and processing capabilities. It achieves top-tier performance in large-block memory write bandwidth at 193.78 GB/sec and single-threaded gzip compression at 40.79 GB/sec. Passmark memory testing places the server in the top 10% with a score of 2989.67. However, a key architectural tradeoff is its shared CPU allocation, which results in average multi-core CPU performance during sustained stress-ng testing, despite strong single-core and cryptographic execution scores.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high resource density, combining a substantial memory footprint with fast NVMe storage on a shared-CPU tier. This makes it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The hardware profile and benchmark results, such as the strong Passmark database operations score of 22098.23 and Redis throughput of 63.9 million operations per second, make this instance well-suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, large-scale web serving, and data decompression tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a memory-optimized server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 32 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD. While the shared CPU allocation represents a performance tradeoff resulting in average multi-core benchmarks, the instance delivers top-tier results in memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression. It also shows strong performance in database operations and Redis throughput. This resource profile offers high memory density and fast local storage on a shared-core tier, making it highly suitable for database hosting, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1920 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a memory-optimized server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring thirty-two AMD EPYC cores, high-capacity NVMe storage, and top-tier memory write bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs with x86_64 architecture and 2.0 GHz clock speed
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD local storage for high-speed I/O operations
Top-tier memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression performance
Shared CPU allocation offering a cost-efficient high-memory profile
Optimized for database hosting, Redis caching, and web serving workloads
